project.name: namba-ai upgrade planning
project.purpose: Create a locked planning contract for upgrading namba-ai so downstream implementation can start from explicit limitations, boundaries, targets, risks, and evaluations without running or modifying namba-ai.

non_goals.title: Do not modify namba-ai in this planning example.
Do not execute namba-ai, Codex, shell adapters, queues, PR automation, or downstream runtimes from this example.
Do not create a SPEC runner or mandatory sequential execution task list.
Do not make NI own namba-ai runtime state; generated outputs remain seed material derived from the lock.

capabilities.title: Capture current namba-ai upgrade limitations as planning evidence
Model SDD sequencing and collaboration issues as graph-oriented planning constraints
Preserve a Codex-limited, pre-runtime planning compiler boundary
Define downstream targets and validation expectations without execution tasks

requirements.title: The plan must state namba-ai's current upgrade-planning limitations: implicit runtime coupling, vague acceptance traceability, sequence pressure, and unclear evidence ownership.
The plan must describe the SDD sequence and collaboration problem as dependency graph planning, not a mandatory linear SPEC chain.
The plan must define a Codex-limited boundary: Codex may consume a compiled prompt, but NI must not invoke Codex or depend on Codex execution state.
The plan must position NI as a pre-runtime planning compiler that locks intent before namba-ai or any downstream harness receives seed material.
The plan must name downstream targets including Codex prompt handoff, namba-ai seed guidance, human-team review, and generated harness proposals.
The generated Codex prompt must be saved under examples/namba-ai-upgrade/generated/ and remain 4000 characters or less.

decisions.title: Use NI as the source-of-truth planning compiler for this upgrade before any namba-ai execution design is accepted.
Represent SDD work as graph-compatible boundaries with dependencies and validation references rather than total-order execution.
Compile the first downstream artifact with ni run --target codex only; do not call Codex exec.

risks.title: Planning may invent namba-ai behavior instead of recording observed limitations and boundaries.
risks.mitigation: Treat this example as upgrade planning only; require downstream implementers to verify namba-ai repository facts before changing namba-ai.

risks.title: A SPEC-style sequence may hide parallel work, collaboration conflicts, or weakened acceptance criteria.
risks.mitigation: Require graph and dependency language, conflict checks, and traceability to CAP/REQ/EVAL/RISK IDs before implementation packets are selected.

risks.title: Codex handoff could be mistaken for NI executing Codex.
risks.mitigation: State that ni run compiles prompt text only; any Codex use is downstream, manual, and outside NI-owned state.

risks.title: NI could drift into a task runner while dogfooding the upgrade plan.
risks.mitigation: Keep non-goals explicit and reject shell adapters, queues, SPEC runners, and execution task creation in this example.

risks.title: Generated seed material may be treated as authoritative after the lock changes.
risks.mitigation: Require generated material to point back to .ni/plan.lock.json and stop with BLOCKED on lock mismatch.

evaluations.title: Limitation coverage review
evaluations.method: Inspect docs/plan/00_project_brief.md and docs/plan/05_constraints.md for the limitation list required by REQ-001.

evaluations.title: SDD graph-boundary review
evaluations.method: Inspect docs/plan/02_capabilities.md, docs/plan/04_domain_state.md, and docs/plan/09_execution_strategy.md for dependency graph language and absence of mandatory execution tasks.

evaluations.title: Pre-runtime boundary review
evaluations.method: Run ni status and inspect docs/plan/03_interaction_contract.md plus generated prompt for no Codex execution, shell adapter, queue, or runtime state ownership.

evaluations.title: Downstream target review
evaluations.method: Inspect docs/plan/08_delivery_operation.md for Codex, namba-ai, human-team, and generated harness targets with derived-only ownership.

evaluations.title: Prompt artifact validation
evaluations.method: Run ni end and ni run --target codex --out examples/namba-ai-upgrade/generated/codex.goal.prompt.txt, then confirm the file is 4000 characters or less.

open_questions.title: Which exact namba-ai repository files should downstream implementation inspect first?
